eyebrowSequence: Made for docs, feedback, and bug reports
hero.title: VivyShot: Screen Studio
hero.subtitle: Built to make screenshots and screen recordings faster for work, docs, bug reports, and AI-agent workflows, with capture, annotation, and export in one place.
hero.meta: Available now on macOS. Windows and HarmonyOS are planned next.

why.title: Why VivyShot
why.subtitle: Simple to use, native on every platform, portable by design, and open source from the start.

native.title: Simple
native.desc: Built for a direct flow: hit <kbd>⌘ ⇧ C</kbd>, select the area, press <kbd>⌘ C</kbd>, and share it.

modern.title: Native
modern.desc: Each platform gets its own native UI, so VivyShot can feel at home wherever it runs.

sync.title: Portable
sync.desc: A shared engine keeps behavior consistent across platforms and makes it easier to bring VivyShot to more devices.

pricing.title: Open source
pricing.desc: The code is public, inspectable, and developed in the open.

now.title: macOS launch
now.desc: Ship the core capture app first.

later.title: More platforms
later.desc: Bring it to more native desktops.

features.title: What you can do with it
features.subtitle: Capture, annotate, record, and export.

rendering.title: Capture screenshots your way
rendering.desc: Start with <kbd>⌘ ⇧ C</kbd>, then capture an area, a window, or the full screen.

connect.title: Mark up the important detail
connect.desc: Use shapes, arrows, text, blur, and pixelate.

pickup.title: Record short flows clearly
pickup.desc: Record screen video with audio and input overlays.

organize.title: Export without extra steps
organize.desc: Use the keyboard to copy fast with <kbd>⌘ C</kbd>, or save clean PNG and JPEG output.

useCases.title: Where it is useful
useCases.subtitle: Useful for bugs, docs, feedback, and AI-agent workflows where a raw screenshot is not enough.

solo.title: Bug reports
solo.desc: Show the bug, the affected region, and the exact visual detail that matters instead of writing around it.

homelab.title: Product feedback
homelab.desc: More clearly call out regressions, layout problems, and polish issues with less back-and-forth.

apple.title: Documentation
apple.desc: Produce screenshots that are easier to read in setup guides, release notes, internal docs, and onboarding flows.

ai.title: AI-agent workflows
ai.desc: Take screenshots for Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, and similar agents when visual context helps them reason better.

pricing.title: Simple pricing
pricing.subtitle: Free, Lifetime, and Supporter. No subscription.

free.name: Free
free.description: A real way to try the app before paying
features: A real way to try it

pro.name: Lifetime
pro.description: A one-time unlock for the full tool

lifetime.name: Supporter
lifetime.description: Same full app, plus a small in-app supporter badge

q1.question: Built for real screenshot work
q1.answer: VivyShot is built to make capture, markup, recording, and export feel fast enough for daily work instead of turning a simple screenshot into a small project.

q2.question: Built to stay native
q2.answer: The goal is native UI on each platform, so the app feels right where it runs instead of forcing one same interface everywhere.

q3.question: Built in the open
q3.answer: VivyShot is open source because tools you use every day should be inspectable, adaptable, and honest about how they work.

q4.question: Built for modern workflows
q4.answer: Use it for bug reports, docs, product feedback, and AI-agent workflows where better visual context saves time and back-and-forth.

closing.title: Available now on macOS.
closing.subtitle: Windows and HarmonyOS are planned next, with native UI on each platform.

footer.tagline: Screen capture, annotation, and recording for macOS
footer.description: Open source and built around a portable engine, with native UI on each platform VivyShot ships on.
